Welcome back to the Long View. I'm Alex Morgan, and my guest is Dr. Eleanor Fisher, a sustainability researcher and systems thinker. Eleanor, welcome. Thank you, Alex. A pleasure. This episode is brought to you by Notion. Notion replaces the dozen tools your team is half-using. Get started at notion.com/podcast. You resist the word sustainability. Why? Because it he is the hard part. Sustainability is a systems problem. You cannot optimize one part without moving another. Fixed emissions in isolation and you push the cost into water, or land, or the grid. The interactions are a story. So it is systems thinking, first and last, manage the cutlings, note the components. When we model it, a decade of interventions, the onestart file it, file it because so many triatea system as a list of parts. And risks it's inside the picture here? The dangers risks are as low as you can name but discount.
